---
title: "Hawaiʻi Coastal Ecosystem Accounting"
subtitle: "Natural Capital Accounts for the Main Hawaiian Islands"
---

## About This Project

This website presents the results of the **Hawaiʻi Coastal Ecosystem Accounting** project, a Pew Marine Fellowship initiative led by the [Oleson Lab](https://www.olesonlab.com/) at the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa. The project develops natural capital accounts for the Main Hawaiian Islands (MHI) following the [UN System of Environmental-Economic Accounting — Ecosystem Accounting (SEEA-EA)](https://seea.un.org/ecosystem-accounting) framework.

Natural capital accounts measure the contribution of ecosystems to human wellbeing in physical and monetary terms, providing a rigorous complement to GDP-based measures of economic activity. For Hawaiʻi, these accounts document the state and trend of coastal and terrestrial ecosystems across traditional land and sea divisions (moku) and quantify the value of ecosystem services — including fisheries — flowing from those ecosystems to society.

---

## Accounts

| Account | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| [**Extents & Conditions**](extents_conditions.qmd) | Ecosystem type areas and biophysical condition indicators by moku (2013, 2016, 2019) |
| [**Fisheries**](fisheries.qmd) | Commercial and non-commercial exchange values by species group, county, and island (1997–2022) |
| [**Recreation**](https://loweas.github.io/CR/) | Non-market values for marine recreational activities |

---

## Data & Reproducibility

All data processing is implemented as a reproducible pipeline using the [`targets`](https://docs.ropensci.org/targets/) R package. Raw data, pipeline code, and processed outputs are version-controlled. To reproduce any result:

```r
# 1. Restore R environment
renv::restore()

# 2. Run the full pipeline
targets::tar_make()

# 3. Render this website
quarto::quarto_render("website")
```

Source code is available in the project repository. Raw data sources are documented on each account page.

### Pipeline graph

Interactive dependency graph for the full project (moku, extents, conditions, fisheries, website).

```{r}
#| label: fig-targets-pipeline-dag
#| fig-cap: "Targets pipeline dependency graph for the full project."
#| fig-height: 7
#| out-width: 100%
#| warning: false
#| message: false
.find_targets_root <- function() {
  starts <- unique(c(
    getwd(),
    if (requireNamespace("knitr", quietly = TRUE) && nzchar(knitr::current_input())) {
      dirname(knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE))
    } else {
      character()
    },
    Sys.getenv("QUARTO_PROJECT_DIR", unset = "")
  ))
  starts <- starts[!is.na(starts) & nzchar(starts)]
  for (s in starts) {
    p <- tryCatch(normalizePath(s, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE), error = function(e) NA_character_)
    if (is.na(p)) next
    for (i in seq_len(20L)) {
      sc <- file.path(p, "_targets.R")
      if (file.exists(sc)) return(normalizePath(p, winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE))
      np <- dirname(p)
      if (identical(np, p)) break
      p <- np
    }
  }
  NULL
}
proj_root <- .find_targets_root()
if (is.null(proj_root)) stop("Cannot find _targets.R (walk up from getwd() or knitr input dir).")
script <- normalizePath(file.path(proj_root, "_targets.R"), winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE)
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(targets)
})
targets::tar_config_set(script = script, store = file.path(proj_root, "_targets"))
owd <- setwd(proj_root)
on.exit(setwd(owd), add = TRUE)
targets::tar_visnetwork(targets_only = FALSE)
```

---

## Acknowledgements

This work was supported by the **Pew Marine Fellowship** and the **University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa**.
